name: LENS @ JHU — Learning Engineering for Next-Generation Systems
affiliation_or_venue: Johns Hopkins University — School of Education / APL
description: Concentration within JHU MEd in Learning Design & Technology. Targets practitioners in complex organizations: defense, healthcare, large-scale education. Grounded in human systems integration and learning engineering expertise. Capstone produces evidence dashboard, reproducible report, and governance/ethics plan. Unique JHU ecosystem: APL + Medicine + IEEE/ICICLE partnership.
notes: Key anchor program. Direct connection to IEEE ICICLE credentialing pathway. Add to seed set for T13, T10.

name: Human-Computer Interaction Institute — CMU
description: Home of Ken Koedinger and Cognitive Tutor / LearnLab. One of the premier academic homes for LE research globally. PhD and MS programs.
notes: Koedinger is co-founder of learning engineering as a named field. Core institutional anchor.

name: Learning Engineering Virtual Institute (LEVI)
description: Funds multi-year cohorts building AI-driven, research-backed tools to improve outcomes in math (LEVI Math) and early literacy (LEVI Literacy), with rapid experimentation and rigorous evaluation. Not affiliated with the earlier iNACOL/Aurora convening sometimes also called LEVI.
notes: Distinct from historical LEVI process-definition work; current org focuses on AI + subject-matter programs. Verify program pages periodically.

name: Open Learning Initiative (OLI)
description: Flagship CMU learning-engineering R&D: course development, instructional training, consulting, and platforms (including Torus) for evidence-based, continuously improving digital learning tied to the Simon Initiative.
notes: Landmark applied LE platform. Seed for T06 and T03. Professional Learning Engineering Fellowship and LearnLab Summer School are listed separately.

name: Ken Koedinger
description: Co-originator of learning engineering as a field. Creator of Cognitive Tutor. Pioneer of PSLC DataShop and LearnLab. Foundational theorist and practitioner.
notes: Must-include anchor person. Seed multiple topics.

name: Ryan Baker
affiliation_or_venue: University of Pennsylvania Graduate School of Education
description: Pioneer in educational data mining and learning analytics; directs the Penn Center for Learning Analytics. Author of key EDM methods handbook. EDM journal editor. Research spans learning engineering, intelligent tutors, and quantitative ethnography.
notes: Key anchor for T04, T06. Pair with LE-PP-084 (Penn Center for Learning Analytics).

name: Bror Saxberg
description: Early champion of learning engineering as a named field. Wrote influential 2016 Atlantic piece calling for learning engineers. Practitioner-bridge figure.
notes: His 2016 article is a canonical entry point to the field. Tier 1 grey lit document.

name: Jim Goodell
description: Co-founder of Learning Engineering book (IEEE Press). Co-editor of canonical LE textbook. Core IEEE ICICLE contributor.
notes: Book co-editor. IEEE Press LE textbook is the primary seed document for the field.

name: John Behrens
description: Evidence-centered design; learning analytics at scale; psychometrics meets LE. Key bridge between assessment science and learning engineering.
notes: Key practitioner for T04, T09.

name: Learning @ Scale
description: Premier venue for LE research at scale. Emphasizes empirical evidence and adaptive/personalized learning. Annual.
notes: Core venue. Must include in expansion sources.

name: Educational Data Mining (EDM)
description: Annual international conference and journal. Primary venue for learning analytics and EDM research.
notes: Key venue for T04, T06.

name: Artificial Intelligence in Education (AIED)
description: Flagship AI in education conference. ITS sub-conference often co-located. Annual; alternating with ITS.
notes: Core venue for T06, T07.

name: Learning Analytics & Knowledge (LAK)
description: Premier analytics in education conference. Strong on data infrastructure and equity.
notes: Core venue for T04, T11.

name: IEEE ICICLE Annual Meeting
description: Annual community gathering for the IEEE learning engineering community. Standards, BoK, and credentialing discussions.
notes: Field-defining community event. T16 seed.

name: ASSISTments
description: Free ITS platform widely used for math tutoring research. Large research base. RCTs conducted on platform.
notes: Classic LE platform. Seed for T06.

name: DataShop / LearnSphere
description: Largest open repository of learning interaction data. Enables corpus expansion and field-level analysis.
notes: Critical infrastructure for LE research community.

name: xAPI / Tin Can API
description: Standard for learning activity data. Foundation for interoperable learning record stores. Key infrastructure standard.
notes: Standards anchor for T11.

name: IEEE ICICLE
description: International Community for IEEE Learning Engineering. Primary professional home for LE. Developing BoK, standards, credentialing. Resources page is a primary seed source for this corpus.
notes: PRIMARY external curation source. Resources page must be fully harvested. SIG/MIG chairs and cadence: see LE-PP-055 and https://sagroups.ieee.org/icicle/sigs/.

name: Society for Learning Analytics Research (SoLAR)
description: International nonprofit dedicated to research, practice, and policy of learning analytics. Home of LAK conference and companion journal.
notes: Key community for T04.

name: International Society for the Learning Sciences (ISLS)
description: Home of CSCL and ICLS conferences. Bridges learning science and design. Important for T01 foundation layer.
notes: Key community for T01.

name: Learning Engineering (IEEE Press textbook)
description: The canonical textbook of the field. Two-part structure. End notes are a primary seed source for citation expansion. IEEE Press, 2022.
notes: PRIMARY seed document. Harvest all chapter end notes for corpus expansion.

name: IEEE ICICLE Resources Page
description: Curated resource list maintained by IEEE ICICLE community. Includes papers, tools, programs, and community resources. Lacks cohesion and learning journey structure — this corpus addresses that gap.
notes: Full harvest required. Categorize each item by content_type and topic_code.

name: How People Learn I & II
description: Foundational synthesis of learning science for educators. Free online. Widely cited. Essential T01 anchor.
notes: Free, authoritative. T1 seed.

name: Saxberg 2016 Atlantic: 'Forgotten Role of Learning Engineer'
description: Canonical entry point essay calling for the learning engineering profession. Widely cited. Short. Often assigned reading.
notes: Tier 1 seed even as grey lit. Critical for T00.

name: ICICLE Body of Knowledge (BoK) — current draft
description: The field's emerging canonical definition document. Defines competency areas, vocabulary, and practice standards.
notes: BoK version tracking required. Update when new version released.

name: IEEE ICICLE SIGs & MIGs
description: Official listing of IEEE ICICLE Special Interest Groups (SIGs) and Market Interest Groups (MIGs): chairs, meeting cadence, and how to subscribe (including ICICLE-CCC and other SIG lists via ListServ@ieee.org per IEEE instructions). Open participation; primary roster of active community leaders beyond the main conference.
notes: Chair/co-chair roster must match sagroups.ieee.org/icicle/sigs/; LE-PP-056–073 are the named SIG/MIG leads from that page.

name: Jodi Lis
affiliation_or_venue: IEEE ICICLE — CCC SIG & Globalisation/Localisation SIG
description: Co-chair of the Competencies, Credentials and Curriculum SIG and the Globalisation/Localisation SIG within IEEE ICICLE. Entry point for credentialing and curriculum alignment discussions in the LE community.
notes: Co-chairs CCC and Globalisation/Localisation per IEEE ICICLE SIGs page. Contact via IEEE roster.

name: Bedriye Akson
description: Co-chair of the Competencies, Credentials and Curriculum SIG (IEEE ICICLE), focused on competencies for learning engineering projects.
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Aaron Kessler
affiliation_or_venue: MIT; IEEE ICICLE — Design for Learning SIG
description: Co-chair of the Design for Learning SIG: human-centered design within the learning engineering process across implementation levels.
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Amy Parent
affiliation_or_venue: Salience Learning; IEEE ICICLE — Design for Learning SIG
description: Co-chair of the Design for Learning SIG (IEEE ICICLE).
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Adesola Ogundimu
affiliation_or_venue: Johns Hopkins University; IEEE ICICLE — Globalisation/Localisation SIG
description: Co-chair of the Globalisation/Localisation SIG: adapting the learning engineering process in local and international contexts.
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Mei-Mei Li
affiliation_or_venue: Arizona State University; IEEE ICICLE — Students and Grads SIG
description: Co-chair of the Students and Grads SIG: mentorship and career development for students and recent graduates in learning engineering and adjacent fields.
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Abby Bregman
affiliation_or_venue: IEEE ICICLE — Students and Grads SIG
description: Co-chair of the Students and Grads SIG (IEEE ICICLE).
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Li (Lee) Liang
affiliation_or_venue: IEEE ICICLE — Students and Grads SIG
description: Co-chair of the Students and Grads SIG (IEEE ICICLE).
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page; name as published on IEEE ICICLE site.

name: Erin Czerwinski
affiliation_or_venue: Carnegie Mellon University; IEEE ICICLE — Tools SIG
description: Co-chair of the Tools SIG (IEEE page lists as chair line; shared leadership with Baptiste Moreau-Pernet): tools for learning engineering practice, data collection, and analysis.
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Baptiste Moreau-Pernet
description: Co-chair of the Tools SIG (IEEE ICICLE).
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Emily Marasco
affiliation_or_venue: University of Calgary; IEEE ICICLE — Higher Education MIG
description: Co-chair of the Higher Education MIG alongside Rob Nyland (IEEE page uses a “Chair” line for the MIG): learning engineering in higher education, instructional design, and research-informed improvement.
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Rob Nyland
affiliation_or_venue: U.S. Air Force / Air University context; IEEE ICICLE — Higher Education MIG
description: Co-chair of the Higher Education MIG (IEEE ICICLE).
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Eric Ultes
affiliation_or_venue: Caemil USA; IEEE ICICLE — Government / Military MIG
description: Co-chair of the Government / Military MIG: learning interventions in government and military contexts.
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Jason Virtue
affiliation_or_venue: Canadian Armed Forces; IEEE ICICLE — Government / Military MIG
description: Co-chair of the Government / Military MIG (IEEE ICICLE).
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: Zarka Ali
description: Co-chair of the pK-12 MIG: learning engineering in preK–12 contexts, instructional design, and analytics-informed improvement.
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: John Ellis
affiliation_or_venue: Dartmouth College; IEEE ICICLE — pK-12 MIG
description: Co-chair of the pK-12 MIG (IEEE ICICLE).
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page; disambiguate from other public figures by ICICLE MIG role and Dartmouth affiliation.

name: John Costa
description: Co-chair of the Workforce MIG: corporate learning and development leaders discussing learning engineering in workplace settings.
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: DJ Jaeger
description: Co-chair of the Workforce MIG (IEEE ICICLE).
notes: Listed on ICICLE SIGs & MIGs page.

name: METALS / MS in Learning Engineering (Carnegie Mellon)
affiliation_or_venue: Carnegie Mellon University — HCII & Psychology / Simon Initiative
description: Graduate program (long known as METALS) training learning engineers in design, learning science, analytics, and intelligent learning systems; capstone with external clients. Public materials may use MS in Learning Engineering naming.
notes: Premier degree pathway explicitly labeled learning engineering. Cross-link with HCII, OLI, and LearnLab.

name: Learning Engineering Fellowship (CMU OLI)
affiliation_or_venue: Carnegie Mellon University — Open Learning Initiative
description: Nine-week intensive for educators and designers to apply learning science and data-informed methods to real educational products and contexts; part of OLI professional learning.
notes: Sister offering: LearnLab Summer School (LearnLab.org / OLI announcements).

name: MIT Learning Engineering and Practice (LEAP) Group
description: Research group applying systems and design methods to STEM and workforce learning; emphasizes AI, simulation, XR, haptics, MOOCs, and curriculum at scale.
notes: Key MIT anchor for learning engineering as a named lab; see group People page for faculty.

name: LearnLab & LearnLab professional programs (CMU / network)
affiliation_or_venue: Carnegie Mellon University — Simon Initiative / LearnLab
description: Research network and professional offerings (e.g., data analysis and modeling workshops, summer school) connecting cognitive science, LearnSphere/DataShop, and course improvement for formal and informal educators.
notes: Complements OLI and METALS; verify current workshop catalog on LearnLab and OLI sites.

name: Siemens Pathway to Learning Engineering
description: Cloud-based, project-based STEM curriculum and educator PD introducing engineering design and industry tools for secondary classrooms; complements university LE pathways.
notes: Industry-sponsored pathway; not a university department.

name: ASU Learning Engineering Institute & Graduate Certificate
affiliation_or_venue: Arizona State University — Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering (Polytechnic / HSE)
description: Graduate certificate and research network fusing human systems engineering, design, and evidence to improve educational systems; connects students with Learning Engineering Research Network partners.
notes: Also see Polytechnic Learning Engineering certificate degree page for credit requirements.

name: Indiana University Luddy — ISE & human-centered computing
affiliation_or_venue: Indiana University Bloomington & Indianapolis — Luddy School
description: Large informatics and engineering school where Intelligent Systems Engineering, human-centered computing, and data-driven design converge—common feeder disciplines for learning engineering careers even where the phrase is not a standalone degree title.
notes: SEED: add specific IU faculty and courses that brand learning engineering explicitly as they are confirmed.

name: John Liu
affiliation_or_venue: MIT Mechanical Engineering / MIT LEAP Group
description: Principal investigator for the MIT Learning Engineering and Practice (LEAP) group; leads work on digital learning, XR/simulation, and STEM workforce education at scale.
notes: Also listed under MIT Open Learning / Digital Learning Lab in institute directories.

name: Norman Bier
affiliation_or_venue: Carnegie Mellon University — Open Learning Initiative
description: Director of OLI; bridges learning science, product, and partnerships for evidence-based online courses and professional programs including learning engineering training.
notes: Anchor industry-facing voice for CMU OLI stack.

name: Scotty Craig
affiliation_or_venue: Arizona State University — Human Systems Engineering / Learning Engineering Institute
description: Professor and leader in ASU Learning Engineering Institute initiatives; graduate program leadership for the learning engineering graduate certificate and related human-systems research.
notes: Verify titles annually against ASU profile.

name: Penn Center for Learning Analytics (UPenn GSE)
affiliation_or_venue: University of Pennsylvania — Graduate School of Education
description: GSE hub for learning analytics and AI in education research, including work that explicitly intersects learning engineering, educational data mining, and design of data-informed learning systems. Related graduate offerings include the online M.S. in Learning Analytics and Artificial Intelligence.
notes: Director: Ryan Baker (LE-PP-011). Primary institutional anchor for UPenn in this corpus.

name: Stanford GSE — M.S. Learning Design and Technology (LDT)
affiliation_or_venue: Stanford University — Graduate School of Education
description: Residential master’s focused on designing and evaluating technology-enhanced learning environments using learning sciences, design methods, and empirical study—one of the clearest Stanford pathways aligned with learning engineering practice (even when the degree title says LDT, not LE).
notes: Cross-links to Stanford HAI education programming where students pursue AI+learning intersections.

name: LIVE Learning Innovation Incubator (Vanderbilt Peabody)
description: Peabody incubator supporting interdisciplinary learning innovation; Peabody teams participate in national AI-for-learning efforts (e.g., AI-ALOE partner work such as intelligent text and workplace learning tools).
notes: No standalone “learning engineering” degree title; anchor for Vanderbilt LE-adjacent R&D.

name: National AI Institute for Adult Learning and Online Education (AI-ALOE)
description: NSF- and Accenture-funded national institute headquartered at Georgia Tech, developing AI theories and systems for adult online learning, intelligent tutors, and workforce upskilling in partnership with universities and industry.
notes: Primary Georgia Tech anchor for AI×learning engineering at scale; consortium includes multiple schools (e.g., Vanderbilt, Harvard).

name: University of Washington — Learning sciences & design (CoE, HCDE, iSchool)
description: Graduate hub in Learning Sciences & Human Development plus related units (e.g., Learning, Epistemology, and Design Lab in HCDE; learning sciences at the Information School) for research on learning, design, and technology in formal and informal settings—UW’s closest cluster to learning engineering even without a single LE degree name.
notes: For HCDE design work see https://depts.washington.edu/ledlab/ (LED Lab); verify program URLs periodically.

name: Purdue School of Engineering Education (ENE)
affiliation_or_venue: Purdue University — School of Engineering Education
description: First-in-the-nation school of engineering education; graduate offerings include the online M.S. in Engineering Education, Ph.D. in engineering education research, and the stackable Teaching and Learning in Engineering graduate certificate—explicit “learning engineering” language appears in certificate and course titles.
notes: Anchor for engineering-education and teaching/learning engineering at Purdue; pair with discipline-specific labs as needed.

name: Herbert A. Simon
description: Nobel laureate (1978) who coined the term 'learning engineer' in the 1967 Educational Record essay 'The Job of a College President.' Argued that teaching effectiveness is a domain of expertise separate from subject-matter knowledge, requiring its own scientific foundation in cognitive science. Co-developed ACT cognitive theory with John Anderson. His work on bounded rationality, human problem solving, and cognitive simulation laid the intellectual foundation for intelligent tutoring systems. The Simon Initiative at CMU is named in his honor.
notes: Founding figure. 'The Job of a College President' (1967) is the field's origin document. Cross-reference LE-PP-100.

name: John R. Anderson
description: Cognitive scientist and architect of ACT-R (Adaptive Control of Thought–Rational), the dominant cognitive architecture for modeling skill learning. Created the LISP Tutor (1983) — the first empirically validated intelligent tutoring system — and co-developed the Cognitive Tutor for algebra, deployed in 2,600+ schools to over half a million students. Demonstrated that ITS-based tutoring produces learning gains equivalent to one-to-one human tutoring (the '2-sigma effect'), providing the empirical foundation for personalized adaptive learning.
notes: Co-originator of Cognitive Tutor and ACT-R. Pair with LE-PP-099 (Corbett) and LE-PP-010 (Koedinger).

name: Allen Newell
description: Pioneer of artificial intelligence and cognitive science. Co-developed SOAR — a unified theory of cognition modeling problem solving and learning through chunking — with Laird and Rosenbloom. With Herbert Simon, created the Logic Theorist (1956) and General Problem Solver (1957), the first AI programs, and established the physical symbol system hypothesis. SOAR's chunking mechanism provides a computational model of procedural skill acquisition that directly informs ITS problem-sequence design and mastery criteria.
notes: SOAR paper (1987, 5000+ citations) is now a corpus seed via LS-SEED-007.

name: John Sweller
description: Educational psychologist who formalized Cognitive Load Theory (CLT), distinguishing intrinsic, extraneous, and germane cognitive load. Established that working memory constraints are a hard design constraint for instruction. CLT-derived principles — the worked examples effect, split-attention effect, and redundancy effect — are among the most replicated findings in educational psychology and are now standard learning engineering design rules. The 1988 founding paper has 4,000+ citations; the CLT literature it seeded spans hundreds of empirical studies.
notes: CLT 1988 paper added as corpus seed LS-SEED-005. Addresses T01 academic paper gap.

name: Ann L. Brown
description: Educational psychologist and pioneer of design-based research (DBR) and collaborative learning. 'Design Experiments: Theoretical and Methodological Challenges' (Journal of the Learning Sciences, 1992) is the most-cited paper in the learning sciences (2,800+ citations), establishing DBR as the canonical methodology for testing engineered learning interventions in authentic classrooms. Co-created Fostering Communities of Learners. Her methodological framework defines the epistemological standards learning engineering uses when validating systems in messy real-world contexts.
notes: DBR 1992 paper added as corpus seed LS-SEED-001. Directly addresses gap g_008 (T17 has zero academic papers).

name: Marlene Scardamalia
description: Pioneer of knowledge-building communities and computer-supported collaborative learning (CSCL). With Carl Bereiter, co-developed Knowledge Forum (formerly CSILE), the first networked collaborative knowledge-building environment. 'Computer Support for Knowledge-Building Communities' (Journal of the Learning Sciences, 1994) has 1,800+ citations and established the socio-cognitive framework for collaborative digital learning platforms — foundational for modern LMS design, MOOCs, and collaborative LE systems. Distinguished by emphasis on collective knowledge construction over individual optimization.
notes: Scardamalia & Bereiter 1994 is in landscape papers (LE-LS-AP-004); represents the constructivist tradition within LE.

name: Chris Dede
description: Pioneer of immersive learning environments and hybrid education. 'Immersive interfaces for engagement and learning' (Science, 2009, 2,000+ citations) established engineering parameters for using VR/AR in STEM education. Co-edited Learning Engineering for Online Education (Routledge, 2019) — the field's first major book — framing online architectures as enabling continuous telemetry that makes LE at scale legible. Post-pandemic work positioned hybrid learning as an optimal long-term state rather than an emergency measure.
notes: Co-editor of the Routledge LE book (LE-LS-GL-008). Pair with LE-PP-009 (Saxberg, also Routledge co-editor).

name: Neil Heffernan
description: Director of Learning Sciences and Technologies at WPI and creator of ASSISTments, a free intelligent tutoring platform serving 500,000+ students and 20,000+ teachers. Built E-TRIALS within ASSISTments — one of the first platforms enabling randomized controlled experiments at scale in live classrooms. Pioneered teacher-authored hint sequences and demonstrated that formative assessment integrated with intelligent hints produces significant learning gains. Research spans educational data mining, learning analytics, and optimization of student learning pathways.
notes: ASSISTments (LE-PP-030) already in registry as TP; this adds Heffernan as person. Key figure for at-scale experimentation.

name: Karen Willcox
affiliation_or_venue: University of Texas at Austin, Oden Institute for Computational Engineering
description: Led MIT's Online Education Policy Initiative (OEPI), producing the 2016 report 'Online Education: A Catalyst for Higher Education Reforms' that re-introduced 'learning engineer' to higher education discourse at the moment MOOCs reached 58M+ students. The report recommended universities create dedicated LE roles and directly catalyzed institutional adoption of LE language. Connected learning engineering to computational science methodologies from aerospace engineering, exemplifying the cross-disciplinary engineering approach the field advocates.
notes: MIT OEPI 2016 report added as LE-PP-101. Cross-reference Karen Willcox as primary author.

name: Albert T. Corbett
description: Co-developer of Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (BKT) with John Anderson — the probabilistic model for tracking per-student, per-skill mastery that remains the most widely deployed student model in production ITS worldwide. Co-designed the ACT Programming Tutor and Algebra Cognitive Tutor. Systematic empirical work on learning curves, error analysis, and mastery criteria established the validation standards for ITS student models. 'Knowledge Tracing: Modeling the Acquisition of Procedural Knowledge' (1994) has 2,500+ citations.
notes: BKT paper (1994) added as corpus seed LS-SEED-003. Pair with LE-PP-091 (Anderson) and LE-PP-010 (Koedinger).

name: The Job of a College President
description: The founding document of learning engineering. Published in 1967 by Herbert Simon in the Educational Record, this essay coined the term 'learning engineer' and argued that teaching effectiveness is a domain of expertise distinct from subject-matter knowledge — requiring its own scientific grounding in cognitive science. Simon proposed that universities employ dedicated learning engineers to collaborate with faculty on evidence-based course design, translating cognitive science into instruction. The philosophical foundation for the field.
notes: Landmark anchor. Landscape source: LE-LS-GL-001. Author: LE-PP-090. CMU digital archive URL.

name: Online Education: A Catalyst for Higher Education Reforms
description: 2016 report by Karen Willcox, Sanjay Sarma, and Philip Lippel that re-introduced 'learning engineer' to higher education discourse at the moment MOOCs reached 58M+ global students. Synthesized advances in cognitive science, neuroscience, education research, and edtech; recommended universities create dedicated learning engineering roles. Widely cited as the document that triggered the modern LE movement in higher education institutions and re-legitimized the Simon 1967 vision in the digital era.
notes: Landmark grey literature anchor. Landscape source: LE-LS-GL-002. Author: LE-PP-098 (Willcox).

name: High-Leverage Opportunities for Learning Engineering
affiliation_or_venue: University of Pennsylvania Center for Learning Analytics
description: 2021 report by Ryan Baker, Ulrich Boser, and Allison Shelley synthesizing a 2020 convening of 100+ academics, policymakers, and practitioners. Defines ten strategic priorities across three domains: Better LE Infrastructure (shared R&D architectures, A/B testing at scale), Supporting Human Processes (teacher dashboards, predictive advising), and Better Learning Technologies (algorithmic equity, complex-skills measurement). The field's most comprehensive contemporary roadmap and the primary policy document cited when framing LE's current agenda.
notes: Field-defining contemporary report. Landscape source: LE-LS-GL-003. Author: LE-PP-011 (Baker).

name: Artificial Intelligence and the Future of Teaching and Learning: Insights and Recommendations
affiliation_or_venue: U.S. Department of Education, Office of Educational Technology
description: 2023 federal policy document addressing AI in education with explicit civil-rights framing. Documented algorithmic bias in e-proctoring systems — facial recognition failures for darker-skinned, transgender, and neurodivergent students; misidentification of neurodivergent behaviors as academic dishonesty. Mandated algorithmic fairness and data representation requirements. Directly relevant to learning engineers designing or procuring AI-driven assessment and surveillance systems. Sets the ethical and legal floor for LE practice in the US.
notes: Primary resource for C34 (Ethics & Equity) — previously empty. Landscape source: LE-LS-GL-004.

name: 7 Things You Should Know About Learning Engineering
description: 2018 practitioner-oriented brief in the canonical EDUCAUSE '7 Things' series, which introduced LE to a broad institutional technology audience including CIOs, provosts, and instructional technology directors. Accelerated LE vocabulary adoption in non-research higher education contexts. Widely cited as the accessible entry point for administrators and practitioners encountering the field for the first time.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-GL-011. High Practitioner Utility score.

name: Learning Engineering Adoption Maturity Model (LEAMM)
description: Organizational capability matrix (2024/2025) allowing enterprises to assess and advance their LE practice across human-centered design, learning science integration, and data-driven iteration dimensions. Provides a structured pathway from ad-hoc instructional design toward mature learning engineering practice. The field's primary organizational self-assessment tool, developed by the ICICLE community including Goodell, Craig, Blake-Plock, and colleagues.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-GL-009. Co-authors include LE-PP-013 (Goodell), LE-PP-083 (Craig).

name: OpenSimon Toolkit (CMU Simon Initiative)
affiliation_or_venue: Carnegie Mellon University, The Simon Initiative
description: The flagship open infrastructure for learning engineering research globally. Comprises DataShop (world's largest open educational interaction data repository), LearnSphere (open learning analytics infrastructure), CTAT (cognitive tutor authoring tools), OLI (Open Learning Initiative courseware), DiscourseDB (NLP for learning interactions), LightSide (automated text coding), and Bazaar (intelligent collaborative chatbot). Enables researchers worldwide to build, test, and study learning engineering systems without proprietary infrastructure.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-GL-010. Pair with LE-PP-031 (DataShop/LearnSphere). This entry covers the full toolkit ecosystem.

name: The Simon Initiative — Carnegie Mellon University
description: Cross-disciplinary learning engineering ecosystem at CMU named for Herbert Simon. Encompasses LearnLab (in-vivo research lab), OLI (courseware platform), DataShop/LearnSphere (world's largest educational log data), METALS master's program, and the OpenSimon Toolkit. Hundreds of faculty; the most integrated LE research-to-practice ecosystem globally. Produces both the theoretical advances and the open infrastructure that defines modern learning engineering.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-CO-002. Institutional home of LE as a named field.

name: Penn Center for Learning Analytics
affiliation_or_venue: University of Pennsylvania, Graduate School of Education
description: Research center directing educational data mining and learning engineering policy work, led by Ryan Baker. Produced 'High-Leverage Opportunities for Learning Engineering' (2021) and several landmark EDM papers. Baker is editor of the Journal of Educational Data Mining and a primary organizer of the EDM conference. Major hub connecting academic EDM research to policy influence and practitioner adoption.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-CO-004. Key policy hub. Pair with LE-PP-011 (Baker), LE-PP-102 (High-Leverage Opportunities report).

name: Journal of the Learning Sciences
description: Founded 1991; the multidisciplinary anchor journal for theoretical models, epistemology, and design-based research in the learning sciences. Home of Ann Brown's 'Design Experiments' (1992, 2,800+ citations) and Scardamalia & Bereiter's knowledge-building paper (1994, 1,800+ citations). Publishes the socio-cognitive and humanistic tradition within LE — essential for grounding algorithmic approaches in rich theories of how people learn in context.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-JO-001. The theoretical anchor journal for learning engineering.

name: Journal of Educational Data Mining
description: Open-access journal dedicated to algorithms and methodologies for analyzing educational data: knowledge tracing, automated feature engineering, predictive student modeling, and behavioral analysis. Baker & Yacef's founding codification of EDM (2009) published here. Premier venue for computational LE methods. Diamond open access — no fees for authors or readers.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-JO-002. Ryan Baker (LE-PP-011) is editor.

name: IEEE Transactions on Learning Technologies
description: Covers technical design and architecture of learning systems: adaptive algorithms, NLP conversational agents, multimodal telemetry (eye tracking, EEG, galvanic skin response), and large-scale platform engineering. Requires substantive technical innovation in computer science alongside pedagogical validity. Key venue for AI-in-education engineering papers and Transformer-based conversational agent research.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-JO-003.

name: Journal of Learning Analytics
description: Official publication of SoLAR. Covers socio-technical implications of educational data: dashboard design, algorithmic ethics, data privacy, human-in-the-loop architectures, and equity in analytics deployment. Complements JEDM's algorithmic focus with practice implications and ethical framing — the two journals represent the LA/EDM distinction that should be preserved in the corpus taxonomy.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-JO-004. Pair with LE-PP-110 (JEDM) — together they define the LA/EDM split.

name: International Journal of Artificial Intelligence in Education
description: Premier venue for ITS, generative AI in education, deep cognitive modeling, and evaluation of AI vs. human tutoring. Covers algorithmic accuracy evaluations, human factors studies, and pedagogical agent design. Craig & Schroeder (2018) design principles for virtual humans published here. The home journal for rigorous AIED research between conference cycles.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-JO-005.

name: Journal of Learning Engineering
description: The only journal specifically dedicated to learning engineering as a named field. Diamond Open Access — no fees for authors or readers. Community-driven by ICICLE; provides the 'Learning Engineering Primer' to align complex multidisciplinary terminology among authors. Because the field is young and sparsely indexed, JoLE papers often do not appear in citation-network-based discovery — venue search is required. Added as a venue query in icicle_adjacent_conference_queries.json.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-JO-006. Critical gap: this journal requires venue-based discovery, not one-hop.

name: International Conference on Intelligent Tutoring Systems
description: The oldest dedicated ITS/LE conference, founded 1988 in Montreal. Biennial; proceedings published by Springer LNCS. Premier venue for cognitive models, student modeling, adaptive instruction, and feedback algorithm research. ITS2026 in Cyprus (June 1–5, 2026). ITS and AIED now alternate years, making one or the other the annual flagship AI-in-education conference.
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-CE-001. Not yet in registry (AIED at LE-PP-022, LAK at LE-PP-023, EDM at LE-PP-021 — ITS was missing).

name: HCII — Human-Computer Interaction Institute, Carnegie Mellon
description: Academic home of learning sciences and technologies at CMU. Houses Ken Koedinger, Al Corbett, and the PACT Center. Offers PhD and MS programs integrating HCI, learning sciences, and data science. Co-originator of learning engineering as a named field; home of DataShop, CTAT, and the Cognitive Tutor research lineage. One of the two most important institutional anchors for LE research globally (alongside the Simon Initiative).
notes: Landscape source: LE-LS-CO-005. Institutional anchor for T06 and T01.
